segments.text:
Levels of Complexity in Living Systems
1  atoms
2  molecules
3  cells
4  tissues
5  organs
6  organisms
7  populations
8  communities and ecosystems
9  biosphere
L102 is mostly #1 - #6
L101 is mostly #6 - #9

atom = element
fundamental unit of matter

different shells = different energy levels
electrons can change shells, but can’t stay between shells/energy levels

atomic number  chemical properties
isotopes = same element, different # of neutrons

C, H, and O make up 90 – 95% of most cells
2
2
+
8
2
+
8
+
8
Filled electron shells = stable

shared electrons -> filled shells
covalent bond

partial positive charge
unequal sharing of electrons -> polar molecule
oxygen is an electron hog
partial negative charge
hydrogen bond – attraction between partial charges

H-bonds form between any
polar molecules

ionic bonds come apart in polar solvents
ions attract partial charges

non-polar molecules – atoms share electrons equally
don’t interact with polar molecules
form “hydrophobic cluster”

Covalent bond – strongest
hard to make and break
takes/releases lots of energy
Ionic bond – medium strength
disrupted by charges, polar molecules/solvents
Hydrogen bond – weakest
Hydrophobic interaction/clustering – not a bond
only happens in polar solvents
